notes:
Turn: openings:plaintiff. Objective: opening statement for proposition that by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace under the market rule.

Record inspection: called wait_for_opportunity first; opportunity ready, id openings:plaintiff. Called get_case and list_evidence. Current record contains five case-packet text files only: market-page, primary-evidence, official-source-record, secondary-reporting, unresolved-record-gaps. No openings, arguments, submitted evidence, exhibits, or technical reports yet. Read all five evidence files in full via read_evidence_range.

Packet analysis: market-page preserves Polymarket event/market metadata and rule text. Rule requires: Iran initiated; non-weather; by listed date 11:59 PM ET; major closure, defined as broad closure/cancellation/suspension for commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or major Iranian airspace region; limited cancellations/delays/partial closures do not qualify; non-weather complete suspension of arrivals/departures at at least two listed airports qualifies. Primary resolution sources are official Iranian aviation authority info and consensus credible reporting. Official-source-record and primary-evidence explicitly state no merits official-source packet has yet been built and identify target sources: Civil Aviation Organization/Authority announcements, NOTAMs, airport-level arrivals/departures for IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN, credible reporting, flight tracking with caution. Unresolved-record-gaps confirms missing UMA proposal/dispute rationale, official records/NOTAMs, event that triggered dispute, timing, Iranian initiation, and whether scope is broad enough.

Native investigation before opening: built source map around official NOTAM/CAAIRI record, airport operations, credible reporting, adverse partial/regional characterization, and exact timing. Web searches run: (1) Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 flights closure Iran aviation authority; (2) site:caa.gov.ir May 24 2026 Iran airspace closure NOTAM; (3) Iran airspace closed May 24 2026 Reuters; (4) Iran suspends flights May 24 2026 airspace; (5) "OIIX A1010/26"; (6) "A1010/26" "OIIX" Iran May 22 2026; (7) "Iran's Civil Aviation Authority" "A1010/26"; (8) "western part of Tehran Flight Information Region" "May 25".

Search leads, not admitted evidence: Multiple leads reference NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, apparently issued May 22, 2026, closing all airports in the western part of Tehran FIR until May 25 and allowing only listed airports/daylight operations/fresh permission. Search result leads included Indian Express, The Kenya Times, Siasat, Defconlevel, AbdiMedia, Facebook/Instagram/OCR snippets, and SafeAirspace overview. These are not admitted evidence and should not be cited as record proof in opening. They are source targets for argument phase.

Adverse checks/limitations: No official CAAIRI NOTAM text captured/admitted yet. Current admitted record does not prove actual May 22-25 closure, exact UTC/ET timing, official initiator, or scope. Potential defense: western-airport/FIR restriction with eight airports still operating daylight-only is a partial or regional restriction, not a major closure; airline/foreign safety avoidance and FAA/EASA warnings are not Iranian-initiated closures. Plaintiff response: market rule expressly allows a major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions; a CAAIRI NOTAM closing all airports in western Tehran FIR with permission-only daylight exceptions would likely satisfy if official text is preserved.

Decision: Opening should be forward-looking, address council, and state that the record already defines the legal test and identifies the missing proof. Do not claim the external NOTAM facts as established record proof. Frame the case theory: decisive proof will be the official NOTAM/CAAIRI record plus credible reporting/airport operations showing a non-weather, Iranian-initiated, broad western-FIR closure effective before the deadline, not merely airline avoidance or foreign warnings. Evidence plan for next phase: submit official NOTAM or authoritative mirror; preserve exact text/timestamps; submit credible reporting with source chain; capture airport/flight operations evidence if needed; seek UMA proposal/dispute rationale.

Turn: openings:defendant. Objective: defendant opening on whether, by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET, Iran initiated a qualifying major non-weather closure of its airspace. Procedure: waited for opportunity first; defendant opening became ready with opportunity_id openings:defendant. Inspected current record with get_case and list_evidence. Record has only five initial case-packet files and plaintiff opening; no submitted merits evidence yet. Statted and read all packet files: ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page, ev_9f8061d601c0_primary-evidence, ev_72c545ccb705_official-source-record, ev_4a5f41c923ac_secondary-reporting, ev_f994792e9085_unresolved-record-gaps. Packet takeaways: market rule requires Iran-initiated, non-weather, major closure by deadline; major closure includes broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing Iranian airspace or major Iranian airspace region, or non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports. Limited cancellations, delays, other partial closures, foreign/airline warnings, and weather closures do not qualify; limited exceptions to broad closure do not defeat qualification. Preferred sources are official Iranian aviation authority information and credible reporting consensus. Existing packet lacks the decisive merits sources: official Iranian aviation records, NOTAMs, airport ops records, contemporaneous reporting, UMA proposal/dispute rationales, exact ET timing, and proof separating Iranian action from airline/foreign-government avoidance. Native investigation before opening: web searches for Iran May 24 2026 airspace closure NOTAM, Civil Aviation Organization Iran, western airspace, eight airports, no new NOTAM, Iran International id 202605238800, Polymarket dispute. Leads found but not admitted: Polymarket event page timeline mentions western Iranian airspace closure, eight-airport/daylight-only restrictions, later reopening, and conflicting characterizations that limited/partial scope did not meet a broader/full major closure test; Gulf News/Indian Express search leads describe western half/sector of Tehran FIR closure May 22-May 25 with only eight airports operational; Iran International/social snippets suggest CAO said no new official aviation warning/NOTAM had been issued; Manifold discussion alleges possible fake/ambiguous news; SafeAirspace gives later June context; UMA oracle page found but web open was dynamic/empty. These are not record support yet and should be captured/admitted in later evidence phases before reliance. Defense theory: plaintiff bears burden; admitted record currently proves the rule and gaps, not the qualifying event. Core disputes are source chain, official Iranian initiation, exact timing, and whether any restriction was broad/major rather than partial western-sector/daylight-only/airport-limited or caused by foreign/airline avoidance. Opening should be forward-looking and record-confined, acknowledge what would satisfy burden, and emphasize that ambiguity/missing official source should count against plaintiff under preponderance.

Turn: arguments:plaintiff. Refreshed record with get_case and list_evidence. Current evidence list initially unchanged from case packet; openings now include plaintiff and defendant. Defendant opening emphasizes gaps, burden, official-source absence, partial/regional limitation, timing, and source-chain issues.

Decisive factual elements: (1) Iran initiated; (2) non-weather; (3) by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; (4) major closure under rule, including broad closure/suspension in major Iranian airspace region, with limited exceptions allowed; (5) source reliability/provenance.

Evidence search and submissions: Used opening source map and web search leads. Opened/captured Indian Express article, Kenya Times article, and Siasat article. Submitted three text captures as evidence.
Accepted evidence IDs:
- ev_98d63ac56d6b_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-98d63ac56d6b: Indian Express captured article extract. Key facts: article published May 23, 2026; states Iran temporarily closed airspace over western half May 22-May 25, suspended all civil flight permits in western sector of Tehran FIR; Iran Civil Aviation Authority issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22 ordering shutdown of all airports in western Tehran FIR effective immediately until May 25; only eight airports operational, including Mehrabad, Imam Khomeini, Isfahan, Shiraz, Yazd, Tabriz, Mashhad, Bandar Abbas; daylight-only and fresh per-flight CAA approval required; commercial aircraft rerouting away.
- ev_5ea126ee410e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-5ea126ee410e: Kenya Times captured extract. Key facts: NOTAM OIIX A1010/26; all airports in western portion of Tehran FIR closed except limited daylight facilities; effective Friday May 22 at 20:46Z until Monday May 25 at 08:30Z; applies broadly to civil aviation activity; prior operator permissions suspended; new approvals from Iran CAA required for IFR passenger operations; limited sites listed OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW.
- ev_ecc6dea2bd69_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-ecc6dea2bd69: Siasat captured extract. Key facts: published/updated May 23; NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 announced closure of airports across western Tehran FIR; limited facilities daylight-only; restrictions until May 25; airlines required fresh approval from Iran CAA before passenger flights.

Provenance/custody: All three are text captures submitted through AAR with source URLs, retrieval timestamp, source description, and relevance. They are secondary-source captures, not official NOTAM artifacts. Indian Express attributes to Gulf News for the NOTAM; Kenya and Siasat do not reproduce an official NOTAM image. Official CAAIRI/NOTAM source remains missing and should be sought in later turns if needed.

Analysis: Three sources independently agree on same NOTAM identifier, issuing authority, affected western Tehran FIR/airports, May 22-May 25 window, CAA approval requirement, and limited daylight exceptions. The Kenya Times gives exact UTC time 20:46Z May 22 to 08:30Z May 25, which is before the May 24 11:59 PM ET deadline. Non-weather reason is security/military escalation; no source indicates weather. Scope is western Tehran FIR/western half of airspace, a major Iranian airspace region. Limited daylight exceptions and a handful of approved airports should not defeat qualification because market rule allows limited exceptions to a broad closure.

Adverse checks/limitations: Current proof is credible secondary reporting but not primary official NOTAM. Defense can argue partial/regional scope and that some airports remained operational. Plaintiff response: market rule covers a major region and limited exceptions; all prior permissions suspended/fresh CAA approval required makes it more than routine partial disruption. Flight-tracking facts are secondary and should not be overemphasized beyond corroboration.

Filing plan: Submit merits argument offering market-page rule and three submitted evidence items. Include technical report/source-search ledger noting official NOTAM not captured, searches and limitations. Argue preponderance, not certainty.

Turn: arguments:defendant. Objective: defendant merits argument. Procedure: after opening accepted, waited until arguments:defendant ready. Inspected record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff submitted three secondary captures: ev_98d63ac56d6b Indian Express, ev_5ea126ee410e Kenya Times, ev_ecc6dea2bd69 Siasat. Read all three via read_evidence_range. Analysis: all are secondary reports, not official NOTAM/CAO source. Indian Express says western half/western sector of Tehran FIR closed May 22-25, civil permits suspended, details attributed to Gulf News/NOTAM; also says eight airports across country remained operational including several market-listed airports with daylight/fresh-approval restrictions. Kenya Times is strongest for plaintiff: NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, effective May 22 20:46Z to May 25 08:30Z, airports in western portion of Tehran FIR closed except limited daylight facilities, prior permissions suspended, new CAA approval for IFR passenger ops, listed OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW. Siasat repeats NOTAM number, western Tehran FIR, daylight exceptions, approval requirement, but lacks exact UTC time. Weaknesses: no official NOTAM text/image, no official CAO publication, possible derivative chain, mixed airport vs airspace language, listed airports under market rule appear operational or exempt rather than completely suspended, commercial transit/arrivals/departures effect inferred. Additional searches run: "OIIX A1010/26" no new NOTAM Iran; "OIIX A1010/26" Iran NOTAM official; "Iran's Civil Aviation Organization" no new NOTAM western airspace; "issued no new official aviation warning" Iran Civil Aviation Organization May 23 2026. Found Iran International article https://www.iranintl.com/en/202605239595 and submitted as defense evidence ev_06f4336a07ad_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-06f4336a07ad. It reports CAO denied issuing a new official aviation notice imposing restrictions, said flight conditions normal/flights operating according to plan, and denied a social-media NOTAM; but it also reports an earlier notice suspending activity at airports in western Tehran FIR until Monday with limited sunrise-to-sunset exceptions. Found Gate News derivative denial article but did not submit because Iran International was better source; found Defcon/social snippets with NOTAM text fragments but not official enough within time. Merits theory: offer DX-1; argue plaintiff did not meet preferred-source burden, source-chain conflict remains, and even face-value reports show partial airport/sector restrictions with numerous daylight exceptions and selected airports operational rather than proven broad closure of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing a major region or complete suspension at two listed airports. Weakness: market rule treats April 2024 western airspace closure as qualifying example, so distinguish by source gap and ambiguous scope, not categorical western-region argument.

Turn: rebuttals:plaintiff. Refreshed prompt and analyzed defendant merits argument. Defendant submitted DX-1 / ev_06f4336a07ad_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-06f4336a07ad (Iran International captured article). I called stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for the full 1554 bytes.

DX-1 exact content: Iran International article titled "Iran denies new NOTAM limiting flights across country," visible metadata May 23, 2026 10:32 GMT+1. It reports Iran's Civil Aviation Organization said it had issued no new official aviation notice imposing restrictions on the country's airspace and that conditions/flights remained normal; spokesperson denied a notice recently circulated on social media. Crucially, it also says the denial came after Iran's CAO said Friday that activity at airports in the western part of Iran's flight information region, known as the Tehran FIR, had been suspended until Monday, with only a limited number of airports allowed to operate. It further says that earlier notice exempted several airports but they could operate only sunrise to sunset.

Rebuttal analysis: Defense uses DX-1 as source-chain conflict. Plaintiff response: DX-1 denies a later/new/circulating-social-media notice but separately confirms the earlier CAO western-Tehran-FIR suspension until Monday with limited sunrise-to-sunset operations. That corroborates rather than defeats the key proposition: a non-weather Iranian CAO restriction in a major airspace region before deadline. The conflict narrows scope and provenance, but preponderance still favors plaintiff when PX-1/PX-2/PX-3 and DX-1 all point to a western-FIR suspension with limited exceptions. Market rule expressly permits major region and limited exceptions. No need to submit additional evidence; use DX-1 itself as visible evidence in rebuttal.

No further native web search performed this rebuttal because the adversary's own exhibit provided the needed context and time is better spent focused on the strongest opposing point. Remaining limitation for closings: official NOTAM/CAA original remains absent; frame as preponderance from corroborated secondary record and adverse-source admission, not primary-certainty.

Turn: surrebuttals:defendant. Objective: answer plaintiff rebuttal. Record inspection: called get_case and list_evidence. No new plaintiff submitted evidence in rebuttal; plaintiff used existing evidence, including DX-1, and filed rebuttal source-conflict analysis. Rebuttal theory: DX-1 confirms the core event and only disputes later/new countrywide or social-media notice; plaintiff says earlier western-FIR suspension with sunrise-to-sunset exceptions qualifies because rule allows major regions and limited exceptions. Defense analysis: plaintiff converts ambiguity into proof. DX-1 reports CAO denial of a new official aviation notice imposing restrictions, normal flight conditions, and flights operating according to plan; its separate earlier-notice sentence refers to airport activity in western Tehran FIR suspended with exempt airports sunrise-to-sunset. That does not prove the rule's broader commercial-flight scope: broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or major region. Rule allows major regions only after breadth is proven; plaintiff still lacks official NOTAM/CAO text, authoritative mirror, affected boundaries, and airport/flight operations data. Listed-airport alternative also fails on current record because plaintiff's own evidence says IKA, THR, IFN, SYZ, MHD remained operational with daylight/fresh approval, not completely suspended. No new evidence submitted for surrebuttal; response will stay narrow.

Turn: closings:plaintiff. Final opportunity: deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission allowed. Per instruction, refreshed record with get_case and list_evidence. Evidence list now includes plaintiff PX submissions and defendant DX-1. No changed metadata that affects closing beyond defendant evidence and surrebuttal already visible.

Record synthesis: PX-0 market rule allows major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions. PX-1 Indian Express: Iran CAA issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 May 22, western Tehran FIR shutdown/airspace over western half, May 22-May 25, only eight airports daylight-only/fresh per-flight approval. PX-2 Kenya Times: exact effective window May 22 20:46Z to May 25 08:30Z; western Tehran FIR airports closed except limited daylight facilities; prior permissions suspended; new Iran CAA approvals required for IFR passenger operations. PX-3 Siasat: corroborates NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, western Tehran FIR, daylight-only, approval requirement. DX-1 Iran International: adverse source says CAO denied a new official aviation notice/social-media notice and normal countrywide conditions, but separately says denial came after CAO said activity at airports in western Tehran FIR had been suspended until Monday with only limited airports sunrise-sunset.

Defense surrebuttal: argues DX-1 has two points; denial of new/countrywide notice matters; earlier western-FIR airport-activity suspension does not establish boundaries/flight categories/broad transit-arrival-departure suspension; official NOTAM remains missing; listed-airport alternative not proven because key airports remained operating; ambiguity remains.

Closing response: Do not rely on listed-airport complete suspension alternative. Rely on major-region route in PX-0. Preponderance does not require official artifact certainty. Four admitted sources, including adverse DX-1, establish an Iranian CAO-imposed western Tehran FIR suspension/restriction before deadline with limited exceptions. Limited exceptions do not disqualify. Non-weather security context. Defense overstates missing official text and treats non-countrywide as non-major contrary to rule.

Unresolved gap to acknowledge: official NOTAM/CAA original not in record; source chain is secondary. But corroboration and adverse-source admission are enough on preponderance.

Turn: closings:defendant. Objective: final defense closing. Record inspection: called get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence after surrebuttal. Plaintiff closing relies on PX-2 as strongest source, PX-1/PX-3 corroboration, DX-1 as alleged confirmation, and PX-0 rule allowance for major region/limited exceptions. Plaintiff closing concedes official NOTAM image missing and separate listed-airport complete-suspension route not proven. Defense closing theory: focus on burden/preponderance, official source gap, source-chain weakness, ambiguity in DX-1, and incomplete breadth proof. Best contrary point is source convergence on western-FIR suspension. Response: convergence is on a broad headline/event, not official operative text or rule-specific breadth; three sources are secondary and one is mixed/denial source. PX-0 preferred sources are official Iranian aviation information plus consensus credible reporting; no official artifact is in record. Major-region clause requires proof of broad closure of commercial flights; evidence mostly says airport activity/airports in western FIR with exceptions and selected airports operational. Limited exceptions cannot substitute for proving broad closure first. Close No.
